# llm.kittens - full documentation bundle for LLM ingestion

This file aggregates the canonical operational documentation and narrative porting notes for llm.kittens.
It is generated from the current repo docs; if it drifts, regenerate it from the listed files.
The browseable index is [docs/README.md](docs/README.md). The single-source-of-truth TODO is [goal.md](goal.md).

The order is:
1. README.md
2. goal.md
3. docs/architecture.md
4. docs/build-and-run.md
5. docs/kernel-reference.md
6. docs/precision.md
7. docs/multi-gpu.md
8. docs/llama3.md
9. docs/testing.md
10. docs/porting-notes.md
11. docs/agents.md

14. doc/attention/attention.md

16. doc/llama3/llama3.md
17. .claude/skills/llm-kittens-port/SKILL.md

================================================================================
## 1. README.md
================================================================================

# llm.kittens

GPT-2 / GPT-3 / Llama-3 training in single-binary C++/CUDA, with every GPU
kernel built on top of [ThunderKittens](https://github.com/HazyResearch/ThunderKittens).
Mirrors [karpathy/llm.c](https://github.com/karpathy/llm.c) feature-for-feature
but replaces its bespoke CUDA kernels (and cuBLAS / cuBLASLt / cuDNN) with
TK-backed ones.

> **In-flight port.** The ⬜ checkboxes in [`goal.md`](goal.md) are the canonical
> "what is done / what is left". Today `make all` builds the GEMM smoke test,
> `train_gpt2cu`, and `test_gpt2cu`; `make gpt2_validate` builds the
> forward-only GPT-2 loss gate; `make train_llama3cu` builds the Llama
> trainer loop with TK GQA forward plus supported-shape backward paths;
> `make test_attention`, `make test_layernorm`, and `make test_attention_gqa`
> build the GPT MHA, GPT LayerNorm, and GQA reference smoke harnesses.
> H100 runtime parity and full Llama-3 support are tracked through M2–M8. **Read [`goal.md`](goal.md)
> first.**

## Documentation

| Need | Page |
|---|---|
| Master TODO and milestone status | [`goal.md`](goal.md) |
| Project shape, layering rules, file map | [`docs/architecture.md`](docs/architecture.md) |
| How to build / run today | [`docs/build-and-run.md`](docs/build-and-run.md) |
| Per-kernel mapping (TK vs verbatim from llm.c) | [`docs/kernel-reference.md`](docs/kernel-reference.md) |
| BF16-only invariant and rationale | [`docs/precision.md`](docs/precision.md) |
| ZeRO + multi-node | [`docs/multi-gpu.md`](docs/multi-gpu.md) |
| Llama-3 (M6/M7) plan and new kernels | [`docs/llama3.md`](docs/llama3.md) |
| Test pyramid + tolerances | [`docs/testing.md`](docs/testing.md) |
| H100 goal validation harness | [`scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh`](scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh) |
| Wrapper-PR checklist and common gotchas | [`docs/porting-notes.md`](docs/porting-notes.md) |
| Narrative kernel porting tutorials | [`doc/README.md`](doc/README.md) |
| Repo-local agent skills | [`docs/agents.md`](docs/agents.md), [`.claude/skills/`](.claude/skills/) |
| LLM ingestion artifacts | [`llms.txt`](llms.txt), [`llms-full.txt`](llms-full.txt) |
| Append-only history | [`CHANGELOG.md`](CHANGELOG.md) |

The full docs index is at [`docs/README.md`](docs/README.md).

## Status snapshot

| Milestone | Scope | State |
|---|---|---|
| M1 | Skeleton, Makefile, verbatim ports, BF16 lock, dataset prep, starter pack | ✅ done |
| M2 | Single-GPU GPT-2 forward (TK GEMM + TK MHA-fwd + TK LayerNorm-fwd + `train_gpt2.cu`) | 🟡 GPT-2 forward wrappers, forward-only validation binary, and MHA/LayerNorm smoke harnesses compile-wired; H100 validation pending |
| M3 | Single-GPU GPT-2 backward + `test_gpt2.cu` numerical parity | 🟡 GPT-2 backward kernels and MHA/LayerNorm smoke harnesses compile-wired; H100 parity pending |
| M4 | Multi-GPU NCCL + ZeRO 0/1, full GPT-2 124M reproduction on 8×H100 | 🟡 scripts + log verifier landed; H100 run pending |
| M5 | GPT-3 variants + multi-node + ZeRO 2/3 | 🟡 descriptor + scripts + GPT dry-runs landed; ZeRO-2 compile-wired; ZeRO-3 parameter-shard runtime path compile-wired; H100/NCCL validation pending |
| M6 | Llama-3 1B (RMSNorm, RoPE, GQA, SwiGLU) | 🟡 converter + kernels + trainer loop + dataloaders/checkpoint-resume/logging compile; TK GQA forward + supported-shape bwd compile; TK tile-load RoPE compile-wired for supported shapes; H100 validation pending |
| M7 | Llama-3 8B multi-node | 🟡 Slurm script + converter validation hooks landed; gated HF conversion/runtime validation pending |
| M8 | Polish, profiling, optional bias-fusion | 🟡 docs + profile/log verifier wired; profile helper enforces the 70% tensor-util gate; parser threshold logic has a synthetic CSV check; GEMM epilogue compile/profile-wired; `ncu`/H100 validation pending |

Per-task checkboxes live in [`goal.md`](goal.md).

## Why

`llm.c` is the reference for "small, fast, single-binary GPT training in pure
C/CUDA" — but its kernels are bespoke CUDA + cuBLAS. ThunderKittens is a
tile-oriented kernel framework from the FlashAttention-3 author, H100/Blackwell
first. Putting them together produces an end-to-end open trainer that uses the
most modern Hopper kernel patterns (WGMMA, TMA) and serves as a worked example
for both projects.

## Hardware

- **H100 (sm_90a)** — primary target. WGMMA + TMA + 228 KB shared mem.
- **RTX 5090 (sm_120)** — supported only for the generic device-test path
  (`rtx5090-device`: CUDA runtime probe plus the plain CUDA SwiGLU smoke).
  The TK model kernels remain H100-only.
- **A100, RTX 4090, B200** — not supported in v1. ThunderKittens has
  experimental sm_100/103/120 support; revisit full Blackwell kernels after v1 lands.

The goal harness enforces this at runtime: `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh
preflight` and `cuda-runtime` target H100/sm90-class devices by default. Set
`DEVICE_TEST_TARGET=rtx5090` for the RTX 5090 device-test probe path, or run
`scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh rtx5090-device`, which forces `DEVICE_ARCH=SM120`.
That path is not valid completion evidence for the unchecked H100 gates in
[`goal.md`](goal.md).

## Precision

BF16 only. ThunderKittens H100 GEMM and MHA kernels are bf16-only and we
inherit the constraint. Master weights, gradient accumulation, and AdamW state
remain FP32 — same invariants as `llm.c`. Compile-time errors out on `ENABLE_FP16`
or `ENABLE_FP32`. Full rationale and the v2 plan: [`docs/precision.md`](docs/precision.md).

## Build (today)

```bash
# Default: assumes ../ThunderKittens is the TK checkout.
make

# Or set TK_ROOT explicitly:
make TK_ROOT=/path/to/ThunderKittens
```

`make` (default) builds `test_matmul`, `train_gpt2cu`, and `test_gpt2cu`.
`gpt2_validate` is the forward-only GPT-2 loss gate; `profile_gpt2cu` is
available as a separate target. `profile_gpt2cu.py --csv-input` exercises the
report parser and tensor-utilization gate from an exported raw CSV, and
`profile_gpt2cu.py --gelu-fusion 1` profiles the opt-in epilogue path. GPT-2's
`-ge 1` flag opts the MLP up-projection into the TK finish-path bias+GELU
epilogue; default remains `-ge 0` until H100 numerics are validated.
`train_llama3cu` builds the
Llama trainer loop with TK GQA forward and supported-shape backward where
available, with CUDA fallback elsewhere. TK-supported GQA shapes rotate Q/K
inside the tile-load path; fallback shapes use fused materialization/unpermute.
The Llama trainer also writes model + per-rank optimizer/dataloader checkpoint
state, writes rank-0 `main.log` metrics, and supports `-y 1` resume from the
latest completed checkpoint. H100 runtime validation remains M6 work — see
[`goal.md`](goal.md).

Requirements: CUDA Toolkit ≥ 12.4, GCC ≥ 11 (or Clang ≥ 14), a ThunderKittens
checkout (header-only), optionally NCCL + OpenMPI for multi-GPU / multi-node
(`NCCL_DIR=/path/to/nccl` or an explicit `NCCL_INCLUDE_PATH` /
`NCCL_LIB_PATH` pair is supported for cluster/module installs),
Python ≥ 3.10 for `dev/data/*.py` preprocessing scripts. Full toolchain detail:
[`docs/build-and-run.md`](docs/build-and-run.md).

## Smoke test (today)

```bash
make test_matmul
./test_matmul

make test_attention
./test_attention

make test_layernorm
./test_layernorm

make test_rope
./test_rope

make test_rmsnorm
./test_rmsnorm

make test_swiglu
./test_swiglu

make test_attention_gqa
./test_attention_gqa
python dev/validate_attention_gqa_reference.py
scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh gqa-runtime
python dev/validate_profile_parser.py
python dev/validate_log_tools.py
python dev/validate_llama3_converter.py --cpp-validate --train-binary ./train_llama3cu
python dev/validate_nccl_source.py
python dev/validate_build_contracts.py
python dev/validate_epilogue_source.py
python dev/validate_gqa_source.py
python dev/validate_runtime_markers.py
python dev/validate_training_source.py
python dev/validate_profile_source.py
python dev/validate_llama_conversion_source.py
python dev/validate_goal_harness_coverage.py
python dev/validate_zero_layout.py

make test_dataloader
./test_dataloader

scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh llama-converter-smoke
scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh llama-checkpoint-smoke

make gpt2_validate
./gpt2_validate
```

The H100 harness asserts the final success markers from these runtime binaries,
including `<binary> smoke OK`, `CUDA runtime check passed.`, `gpt2_validate OK`,
and `test_gpt2cu OK`, rather than relying only on process exit status.
Use `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh host-core` on local machines that can compile
and run host-only checks but do not have a working CUDA runtime.
Use `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh rtx5090-device` on an RTX 5090 host to build
the generic CUDA probes with `DEVICE_ARCH=SM120` and run the CUDA runtime plus
plain CUDA SwiGLU smoke. `ALLOW_NON_H100=1` may still be used with the
`preflight` and `cuda-runtime` probes only for dry debugging on unsupported GPUs;
real runtime gates still require H100/sm90-class hardware.
`goal-complete` refuses to run with that override set.
On a target machine where every remaining `goal.md` gate should run in one
intentional pass, use
`ALLOW_FULL_GOAL_RUN=1 scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh goal-complete`; this runs
the ZeRO-3 smoke plus long H100/NCCL/profile/conversion/full-run phases after
`goal-core`.
That completion pass requires `GPT2_FULL_EXPECTED_VAL_LOSS` and
`GPT2_FULL_EXPECTED_HELLASWAG` so the GPT-2 full reproduction is checked against
explicit published metrics, plus explicit max/min thresholds for the GPT-2
smoke, ZeRO-3 GPT-2 smoke, GPT-2 two-node comparison, Llama resume,
Llama-3 1B stability/full, and Llama-3 8B full phases. GPT-2 smoke, ZeRO-3
smoke, Llama resume, and long training phases
validate rank-0 `main.log` with `dev/validate_training_log.py` after launch;
full-run gates also require captured `run.log` launch metadata and final
checkpoint markers/artifacts.
`gpt2-two-node` runs the filesystem Slurm script for 100 steps by default and
compares the single-node vs two-node train-loss curves with
`dev/compare_training_logs.py`, requiring both curves to decrease over the
compared window. Llama resume and the 8B Slurm gate
also header-validate the expected checkpoint artifacts. The 8B Slurm gate uses
`sbatch --wait` unless `LLAMA8B_FULL_VALIDATE_ONLY=1` is set for an existing
output directory.

Sweeps three forward GEMM shapes (a 1024³ square, the GPT-2 124M MLP
up-projection, and the GPT-2 124M LM head), the opt-in forward bias+GELU
epilogue shape, plus both dWeight `A^T*B` paths: overwrite and accumulated
`+=` through the scratch-backed TK path. All compare against naive bf16
references with FP32 accumulation. Requires an H100 to be
meaningful at runtime.

`test_attention` checks GPT-style packed Q/K/V attention against an independent
CPU reference, including direct TK forward, padded TK forward, fallback
backward, and supported-shape TK backward.

`test_layernorm` checks GPT LayerNorm forward, fused residual+LayerNorm forward,
saved `mean`/`rstd`, and backward `+=` accumulation into `dinp`, `dweight`, and
`dbias` against independent CPU references.

## Quickstart (compile-ready, runtime parity pending)

```bash
# 1. Reference checkpoints + tokenizer for GPT-2 124M.
./dev/download_starter_pack.sh

# 2. Tokenize a smoke-test dataset.
python dev/data/tinyshakespeare.py

# 3. Build the trainer.
make train_gpt2cu

# 4. Train 100 steps from the bf16 124M checkpoint.
./train_gpt2cu \
    -i dev/data/tinyshakespeare/tiny_shakespeare_train.bin \
    -j dev/data/tinyshakespeare/tiny_shakespeare_val.bin \
    -e gpt2_124M_bf16.bin \
    -b 4 -t 1024 -x 100 -v 20 -s 0
```

For the full GPT-2 124M reproduction on 8×H100 (M4 milestone), see
`scripts/run_gpt2_124M.sh`. The single-node and multi-node script list is in
[`docs/multi-gpu.md`](docs/multi-gpu.md#per-script-status).

## Layout

```
llm.kittens/
├── train_gpt2.cu          GPT-2 / GPT-3 entrypoint  [compile-ready; runtime parity pending]
├── train_gpt2.py          PyTorch reference / .bin exporter (verbatim)
├── train_llama3.cu        Llama-3 trainer/checkpoint-resume [compile-ready; H100 validation pending]
├── train_llama3.py        Llama-3 HF→.bin converter
├── dev/validate_gpt2_starter_pack.py Host-only GPT-2 artifact checker
├── dev/validate_data_artifacts.py Host-only GPT-2/Llama train/eval data checker
├── dev/validate_attention_gqa_reference.py CPU-only GQA/RoPE PyTorch reference checker
├── dev/validate_profile_parser.py Host-only profile CSV parser/threshold checker
├── dev/validate_llama3_converter.py Host-only Llama write_model header/payload checker
├── dev/validate_nccl_source.py Host-only NCCL/ZeRO runtime-contract source guard
├── dev/validate_build_contracts.py Host-only BF16/H100/TK build-contract source guard
├── dev/validate_epilogue_source.py Host-only GPT-2 bias+GELU epilogue/profile source guard
├── dev/validate_gqa_source.py Host-only GQA/RoPE routing and gradient-unpermute source guard
├── dev/validate_runtime_markers.py Host-only runtime success-marker source guard
├── dev/validate_training_source.py Host-only rank-0 training-log evidence source guard
├── dev/validate_profile_source.py Host-only Nsight Compute profile-gate source guard
├── dev/validate_llama_conversion_source.py Host-only Llama-3.1 8B conversion source guard
├── dev/validate_goal_harness_coverage.py Host-only compile/goal-complete coverage guard
├── dev/test_dataloader.cpp Host-only GPT-2/Llama DataLoader/EvalLoader smoke
├── test_gpt2.cu           Numerical parity test     [compile-ready; runtime parity pending]
├── profile_gpt2.cu        ncu profiling target      [compile-ready; supports --gelu-fusion 0|1; runtime pending]
├── llmc/                  Kernel + utility headers (C-style API)
│   ├── matmul.cuh         TK-backed GEMM fwd + dInp/dWeight/dbias backward
│   ├── attention.cuh      TK MHA fwd/bwd; padded fwd dispatch; CUDA bwd fallback
│   ├── attention_gqa.cuh  Llama-3 GQA + RoPE attention (TK fwd/supported bwd; CUDA fallback)
│   ├── layernorm.cuh      TK LayerNorm fwd + fused-residual; TK warp-sum bwd reductions
│   ├── rmsnorm.cuh        Llama-3 RMSNorm (TK fwd + fused residual; CUDA bwd)
│   ├── rope.cuh           Llama-3 rotary embeddings (TK; compile-ready)
│   ├── encoder.cuh        token+position embedding (verbatim)
│   ├── gelu.cuh           GELU (verbatim)
│   ├── swiglu.cuh         Llama-3 SwiGLU activation (plain CUDA)
│   ├── fused_classifier.cuh  cross-entropy + softmax (verbatim)
│   ├── adamw.cuh          AdamW step (verbatim)
│   ├── global_norm.cuh    grad-clip norm (verbatim)
│   ├── zero.cuh           NCCL + ZeRO-0/1/2/3; ZeRO-3 parameter shards all-gather into the full compute layout
│   ├── dataloader.h      GPT-2/Llama train+eval loaders (header-dispatched)
│   ├── tokenizer.h, sampler.h, schedulers.h, ...   (verbatim)
│   └── tk/                ThunderKittens template-heavy implementations
│       ├── tk_common.cuh  Bridge: bf16 alias, TK_ALIGN, smem helper, BF16 static_assert
│       ├── gemm_h100.cuh  TK bf16 GEMM in header form (✓)
│       ├── attention_h100.cuh   TK MHA fwd (T%192) + bwd (T%256) H100 kernels
│       ├── attention_gqa_h100.cuh   TK GQA fwd + supported bwd [M6 partial; tile-load RoPE compile; runtime pending]
│       ├── layernorm_tk.cuh     TK LayerNorm forward + fused-residual (✓)
│       ├── rmsnorm_tk.cuh       TK RMSNorm forward + fused-residual wrapper (✓ compile-ready)
│       └── rope_tk.cuh          TK RoPE wrapper (✓ compile-ready)
├── dev/
│   ├── cuda/test_matmul.cu         GEMM smoke test (✓)
│   ├── cuda/test_attention.cu      GPT MHA smoke harness (✓ compile; runtime pending)
│   ├── cuda/test_layernorm.cu      GPT LayerNorm smoke harness (✓ compile; runtime pending)
│   ├── cuda/test_rope.cu           RoPE smoke harness (✓ compile; runtime pending)
│   ├── cuda/test_rmsnorm.cu        RMSNorm smoke harness (✓ compile; runtime pending)
│   ├── cuda/test_swiglu.cu         SwiGLU smoke harness (✓ compile; runtime pending)
│   ├── cuda/test_attention_gqa.cu  GQA + RoPE smoke harness (✓ compile; runtime pending)
│   ├── data/                  dataset preprocessing (mirror of llm.c/dev/data/)
│   ├── test_dataloader.cpp    host-only GPT-2/Llama DataLoader/EvalLoader smoke
│   ├── download_starter_pack.sh
│   ├── validate_data_artifacts.py  host-only prepared-data artifact validator
│   ├── validate_attention_gqa_reference.py  CPU-only GQA/RoPE PyTorch reference check
│   ├── validate_profile_parser.py  host-only profile CSV parser/threshold check
│   ├── validate_llama3_converter.py  host-only Llama write_model header/payload smoke
│   └── download_llama3.py     gated HF Llama-3 converter wrapper + synthetic checkpoint validator
├── scripts/                   Single-node + multi-node training scripts  [GPT/GPT-3/Llama scripts ported; runtime pending]
│   └── validate_goal_h100.sh   H100 validation harness for remaining goal gates
├── docs/                      Operational documentation
└── doc/                       In-tree tutorial / porting notes  [present; runtime notes still pending]
```

The rule for `llmc/`: every wrapper in `llmc/*.cuh` exposes a C-style function
signature. Only files under `llmc/tk/` `#include <kittens.cuh>` and use the
`kittens::` namespace. This keeps `train_*.cu` free of template noise. Full
explanation in [`docs/architecture.md`](docs/architecture.md).

## What changed vs. llm.c

| Component | llm.c | llm.kittens |
|---|---|---|
| Matmul | cuBLASLt (with bias + GELU epilogue fusion) | TK H100 bf16 GEMM (`A*B^T` model-weight path); default bias/GELU are separate passes, with opt-in GPT-2 MLP bias+GELU epilogue compile-wired behind `-ge 1` |
| Attention forward | cuDNN flash-attn or fallback CUDA | TK MHA forward (bf16, head_dim ∈ {64, 128}) |
| Attention backward | cuDNN flash-attn-bwd | TK MHA backward |
| LayerNorm forward | hand-written CUDA | TK layernorm (forked, dropout removed, `D` re-templated) |
| LayerNorm backward | hand-written CUDA | hand-written CUDA accumulator with TK warp reductions |
| Encoder, GELU, SwiGLU, fused_classifier, AdamW, global_norm | plain CUDA | plain CUDA, **kept verbatim from llm.c** |
| ZeRO / multi-node init | plain CUDA + NCCL | ZeRO-0/1/2/3 and NCCL init wired; ZeRO-3 parameter shards all-gather into the current full compute layout; H100/NCCL validation pending |
| Precision | BF16 / FP16 / FP32 | BF16 only |
| Allocator alignment | 16 bytes | 128 bytes (TMA descriptors) |
| Build | nvcc + cuBLAS + cuBLASLt + (optional) cuDNN | nvcc + TK headers, single binary |

The principle: **TK earns its keep on tile-MMA ops** (GEMM, MHA, LayerNorm).
Everywhere else, `llm.c`'s plain CUDA kernels are kept verbatim — TK adds
nothing on element-wise / gather-scatter / 1D-reduction workloads.

## License

MIT, matching `llm.c` and ThunderKittens.

## Acknowledgements

- [karpathy/llm.c](https://github.com/karpathy/llm.c) — the structural template
  for this project. Most of `llmc/*` is ported verbatim.
- [HazyResearch/ThunderKittens](https://github.com/HazyResearch/ThunderKittens)
  — the kernel framework everything tile-shaped is built on.
================================================================================
## 2. goal.md
================================================================================

# Goal — port `llm.c` onto ThunderKittens, feature-for-feature

This file is the **single source of truth** for what is done and what is left.
Every docs page links here. Every PR should tick a box.

The shape of the finished project is laid out in [`docs/architecture.md`](docs/architecture.md).
The kernel-by-kernel mapping is in [`docs/kernel-reference.md`](docs/kernel-reference.md).
History of what changed and when is in [`CHANGELOG.md`](CHANGELOG.md).

## Status at a glance

| Milestone | Scope | State |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| **M1** | Skeleton, Makefile, verbatim ports, BF16 lock, dataset prep, starter-pack download | ✅ done |
| **M2** | Single-GPU GPT-2 forward (TK GEMM + TK MHA-fwd + TK LayerNorm-fwd + adapted `train_gpt2.cu`) | 🟡 partial — GPT-2 forward wrappers, forward-only validation binary, and MHA/LayerNorm smoke harnesses are compile-wired; H100 forward-loss validation still pending |
| **M3** | Single-GPU GPT-2 backward + AdamW + numerical parity (`test_gpt2.cu`) | 🟡 partial — GPT-2 backward kernels, AdamW, global norm, MHA/LayerNorm smoke harnesses, and parity harness compile; H100 parity still pending |
| **M4** | Multi-GPU NCCL + ZeRO 0/1, full GPT-2 124M reproduction on 8×H100 | 🟡 scripts + log verifier landed; H100 run pending |
| **M5** | GPT-3 variants + multi-node (MPI/TCP/FS) + ZeRO 2/3 | 🟡 descriptor + scripts + GPT dry-runs landed; ZeRO-2 compile-wired; ZeRO-3 parameter-shard runtime path compile-wired; H100/NCCL validation pending |
| **M6** | Llama-3 1B (RMSNorm, RoPE, GQA attention, SwiGLU) | 🟡 converter + kernels + trainer loop + dataloaders/checkpoint-resume/logging compile; TK GQA forward + supported-shape bwd compile; TK tile-load RoPE compile-wired for supported shapes; H100 validation pending |
| **M7** | Llama-3 8B multi-node | 🟡 Slurm script + converter validation hooks landed; gated HF conversion/runtime validation pending |
| **M8** | Polish: docs index, profiling output, optional v1.1 cuBLAS-style bias-fusion in TK GEMM | 🟡 docs + profile/log verifier wired; profile helper enforces the 70% tensor-util gate; parser threshold logic has a synthetic CSV check; GEMM epilogue compile/profile-wired; `ncu`/H100 validation pending |

Legend: ✅ done · 🟡 partial · ⬜ not started.

---

## M1 — Skeleton (✅ done)

- [x] `Makefile` — `sm_90a`, `c++20`, `--use_fast_math`, TK include paths, BF16-only, NCCL/MPI optional, GPU-capability sniff with warning. See [`docs/build-and-run.md`](docs/build-and-run.md).
- [x] `llmc/cuda_common.h` — `floatX = __nv_bfloat16` locked; compile error if `ENABLE_FP16`/`ENABLE_FP32` is defined.
- [x] `llmc/cuda_utils.cuh` — `x128`, `f128`, `stochastic_rounding` ported verbatim from llm.c.
- [x] `llmc/cublas_common.h` — kept as a stub; cuBLAS is removed in v1.
- [x] Verbatim element-wise ports from llm.c: `encoder.cuh`, `gelu.cuh`, `fused_classifier.cuh`, `adamw.cuh`, `global_norm.cuh`.
- [x] `zero.cuh` — NCCL + ZeRO-0/1/2/3 and MPI/TCP/FS init are wired. ZeRO-3 owns a local parameter shard, updates that shard, and all-gathers into the existing full compute layout; full compute and gradient buffers are still allocated by the current kernels.
- [x] Utility headers: `dataloader.h` (extended to dispatch GPT-2 uint16 and Llama-3 uint32 training/eval files by header magic), plus verbatim `tokenizer.h`, `sampler.h`, `schedulers.h`, `rand.h`, `mfu.h`, `outlier_detector.h`, `logger.h`, `utils.h`.
- [x] `llmc/tk/tk_common.cuh` — `bf16` aliases, `TK_ALIGN = 128` (TMA-aligned allocator constant), `tk_set_max_dynamic_smem`, hard `static_assert` on `floatX == __nv_bfloat16`, hard `#error` if `KITTENS_SM90` is undefined.
- [x] `dev/data/` — full mirror of `llm.c/dev/data/` (`tinyshakespeare.py`, `tinystories.py`, `fineweb.py`, `fineweb.sh`, `edu_fineweb.sh`, `hellaswag.py`, `mmlu.py`, `data_common.py`, `README.md`). `dev/validate_data_artifacts.py` validates prepared GPT-2/Llama train and eval `.bin` metadata without CUDA; `--self-test` writes synthetic GPT/Llama train/eval artifacts and checks expected parser failures.
- [x] `dev/download_starter_pack.sh` — fetches `gpt2_tokenizer.bin`, `gpt2_124M.bin`, `gpt2_124M_bf16.bin`, `gpt2_124M_debug_state.bin` from Karpathy's HF mirror. `dev/validate_gpt2_starter_pack.py` verifies the tokenizer, fp32/BF16 checkpoint metadata, and debug-state payload shape/size without CUDA; `--self-test` writes tiny synthetic starter-pack artifacts and checks expected parser failures.
- [x] `requirements.txt` — `tqdm`, `numpy<2`, `torch`, `tiktoken`, `transformers`, `datasets`, `requests`.
- [x] `profile_gpt2cu.py` — nsight-compute post-processing helper, adapted to the TK-only build flags. It supports `--csv-input` for host-only parser/threshold validation without invoking `ncu`, and `--gelu-fusion 0|1` to profile the default path or the opt-in GPT-2 MLP bias+GELU epilogue.

## M2 — Single-GPU GPT-2 forward (🟡 partial)

What is done:

- [x] `llmc/tk/gemm_h100.cuh` — TK bf16 H100 GEMM ported into header form. Specialisations cover both `A*B` and `A*B^T`; the `A*B^T` path is used for llm.c model weights stored as `(OC, C)`. Two N-block sizes are exposed: `matmul_default<2,4,8>` (N % 256, default) and `matmul_small_n<2,2,8>` (N % 128, used by the GPT-2 LM head). Persistent grid (132 SMs), TMA producer, WGMMA consumer.
- [x] `llmc/matmul.cuh` — C-style `matmul_forward(out, inp, weight, bias, B, T, C, OC, stream)`. Dispatches to the TK `A*B^T` GEMM based on `OC % 256`, matching llm.c's `(OC, C)` weight layout. The default path applies bias in a separate pass (`add_bias_kernel`); the GPT-2 MLP up-projection can opt into the compile-wired TK finish-path bias+GELU epilogue through `matmul_forward_gelu` / `-ge 1`, pending H100 numerical validation.
- [x] `llmc/matmul.cuh` — `matmul_backward_bias_kernel9` and `reduce_add_sum_kernel` ported verbatim from llm.c. They are the column-reduction kernels the M3 backward will call.
- [x] `llmc/tk/attention_h100.cuh` + `llmc/attention.cuh` — TK MHA forward fast path for `T % 192 == 0`, plus padded TK dispatch for other sequence lengths (including GPT-2 `T=1024`). Keeps the verbatim llm.c QKV permute/unpermute glue.
- [x] `llmc/layernorm.cuh` — llm.c LayerNorm / fused residual / backward correctness baseline ported so the GPT-2 trainer API compiles. Forward and fused-residual forward now dispatch to the TK fork for supported widths; CUDA baseline remains as fallback.
- [x] `llmc/tk/layernorm_tk.cuh` — TK LayerNorm forward fork compile-wired for widths `{768, 1024, 1280, 1600, 2048, 4096}`. Dropout stripped, internal sync removed, `cudaStream_t` launchers added, and `mean`/`rstd` saved for GPT-2 backward.
- [x] `train_gpt2.cu` — ported from llm.c, cuBLAS/cuDNN paths stripped, matmul/attention/layernorm call sites routed through local wrappers, default GELU path kept as explicit `matmul_forward` + `gelu_forward`, 128-byte parameter-offset assertions added, CLI preserved. `-ge 1` now opts GPT-2 MLP up-projection into the TK finish-path bias+GELU epilogue; default remains `0` until H100 numerics are validated.
- [x] `dev/cuda/test_matmul.cu` — GEMM smoke test. Sweeps three forward shapes (1024³ square, GPT-2 124M MLP up `4096×3072×768`, GPT-2 124M LM head `4096×50304×768`), the opt-in forward bias+GELU epilogue shape, plus overwrite and accumulated dWeight `A^T*B` shapes, comparing against naive bf16 references with FP32 accumulation. Tolerance `0.5` (loose because bf16 + K=768/1024).
- [x] `dev/cuda/test_attention.cu` — GPT-style MHA smoke harness with an independent CPU reference for packed Q/K/V, causal forward, and packed Q/K/V gradients. It covers direct TK forward with fallback backward at `T=192`, and padded TK forward plus supported-shape TK backward at `T=256`. Compile-checked with `make test_attention NO_MULTI_GPU=1 NO_USE_MPI=1 FORCE_NVCC_O=0`; runtime is blocked locally by the CUDA driver/runtime mismatch.
- [x] `dev/cuda/test_layernorm.cu` — GPT-style LayerNorm smoke harness with an independent CPU reference for forward, fused residual+LayerNorm forward, saved `mean`/`rstd`, and backward `+=` accumulation into `dinp`, `dweight`, and `dbias`. Compile-checked with `make test_layernorm NO_MULTI_GPU=1 NO_USE_MPI=1 FORCE_NVCC_O=0`; runtime is blocked locally by the CUDA driver/runtime mismatch.
- [x] `dev/cuda/gpt2_validate.cu` + `make gpt2_validate` — forward-only M2 validation binary. It loads `gpt2_124M_debug_state.bin`, runs `gpt2_validate()` on the reference batch, and checks the forward loss without entering backward or AdamW. Compile-checked with `make gpt2_validate NO_MULTI_GPU=1 NO_USE_MPI=1 FORCE_NVCC_O=0`; runtime is blocked locally by the CUDA driver/runtime mismatch.
- [x] `make all` — builds `test_matmul`, `train_gpt2cu`, and `test_gpt2cu` with the current compile-ready GPT-2 path.

What remains:

- [x] **Finish TK MHA forward wrapper** — `llmc/attention.cuh` + `llmc/tk/attention_h100.cuh`.
  - Port from `ThunderKittens/kernels/attention/mha_h100/mha_h100.cu` (`fwd_attend_ker`).
  - Keep llm.c's `permute_kernel` / `unpermute_kernel` (`llm.c/llmc/attention.cuh:14-83`) so QKV layout `(B,T,3,NH,HS)` becomes the `(B,NH,T,HS)` TK kernel expects.
  - Constraint: TK MHA `head_dim ∈ {64, 128}`. GPT-2 = 64, Llama-3 = 128. Anything else needs a new TK specialization.
  - Match llm.c's wrapper signature at `llm.c/llmc/attention.cuh:195`.
  - Non-192-aligned lengths are padded in scratch, run through TK at `Tpad`, then unpadded into the normal output.
  - Verification so far: compile-only (`make all`, `make test_attention`). H100 numerical validation remains covered by `gpt2_validate` below.
- [x] **TK LayerNorm forward wrapper** — `llmc/layernorm.cuh` + `llmc/tk/layernorm_tk.cuh`.
  - Fork `ThunderKittens/kernels/layernorm/layernorm.cu`. Re-template the hidden width `D` over GPT-2 / Llama widths {768, 1024, 1280, 1600, 2048, 4096}. Strip the dropout path. Remove the internal `cudaDeviceSynchronize`. Add a `cudaStream_t` parameter.
  - Also expose `fused_residual_forward5` matching `llm.c/llmc/layernorm.cuh:467`.
  - Verification so far: compile-only (`make all`, `make test_layernorm`). H100 numerical validation remains covered by `gpt2_validate` below.
- [ ] `./gpt2_validate` returns a sane forward loss against `gpt2_124M_debug_state.bin` on H100 (forward only — 10-step optimizer match comes in M3).

## M3 — Single-GPU GPT-2 backward (🟡 partial)

- [x] **TK MHA backward** wrapper in `llmc/attention.cuh` + `llmc/tk/attention_h100.cuh`. Ported `bwd_attend_prep_ker` + `bwd_attend_ker` from `ThunderKittens/kernels/attention/mha_h100/mha_h100.cu`. The fast path supports `head_dim ∈ {64, 128}` and `T % 256 == 0`, uses the forward-saved LSE in `att`, and falls back to the slow CUDA recompute baseline for unsupported shapes. `dev/cuda/test_attention.cu` compile-wires a CPU-reference forward/backward smoke harness; H100 numerical parity still pending.
- [x] **Hand-written LayerNorm backward** in `llmc/layernorm.cuh`. `layernorm_backward_kernel10` keeps the llm.c cross-block atomic-counter accumulator pattern and uses a TK `kittens::warp::sum` shared-vector helper for the row-wise backward reductions. `dev/cuda/test_layernorm.cu` compile-wires a CPU-reference forward/fused/backward smoke harness; H100 numerical parity still pending.
- [x] **TK `matmul_backward`** in `llmc/matmul.cuh`.
  - `dInp = dOut · W` is wired through the existing TK `A*B` GEMM because `W` is stored `(OC, C)`.
  - `dWeight = dOutᵀ · Inp` uses TK `A^T*B` when the destination is known to be zero (`micro_step == 0`).
  - Accumulated `dWeight += dOutᵀ · Inp` uses a dedicated aligned `matmul_scratch` activation buffer for the TK product, then adds the temporary result into the gradient tensor. The slow CUDA `+=` kernel remains only as a fallback for unsupported shapes or missing scratch.
  - `dBias = column-reduction of dOut` uses `matmul_backward_bias_kernel9` + `reduce_add_sum_kernel` when the auxiliary buffer is available.
- [x] **AdamW step** wired through (`llmc/adamw.cuh` already has the kernel; just call it from `train_gpt2.cu`).
- [x] **`global_norm` grad-clip** wired through.
- [x] **`test_gpt2.cu`** ported from `llm.c/test_gpt2.cu` and compile-checked. Loads `gpt2_124M_debug_state.bin`, runs forward + 10 AdamW steps, compares against the PyTorch reference.
  - The gradient tolerance table is now explicit in `test_gpt2.cu`, with inherited llm.c BF16 thresholds, current TK thresholds, tensor names, and notes for tensors likely to move after the first H100 TK MHA-bwd parity run.
- [ ] `make test_gpt2cu && ./test_gpt2cu` passes on H100.

## M4 — Multi-GPU GPT-2 124M reproduction (🟡 partial)

- [x] Wire `zero.cuh` into the `train_gpt2.cu` we land in M2 — NCCL init, ZeRO-0 and ZeRO-1 sharding, gradient all-reduce, master-weight broadcast. Compile-wired; distributed runtime still needs H100/NCCL validation.
- [x] `scripts/run_gpt2_124M.sh` — port from `llm.c/scripts/run_gpt2_124M.sh`. B=64, T=1024, LR=6e-4, warmup=700, cosine→0, 18,865 steps. ZeRO-1. The script passes `-x "$MAX_STEPS"`, writes `run.log` launch metadata, and derives `DONE_*` from the same value so the harness final-step check matches the launched run.
- [ ] 8×H100 end-to-end: final HellaSwag accuracy and val loss within 0.5% of llm.c's published numbers. `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh gpt2-full` now verifies the rank-0 `main.log` final metrics, `run.log` launch metadata, and final checkpoint marker/artifacts after launch; `GPT2_FULL_VALIDATE_ONLY=1` validates existing completed evidence instead of relaunching. `goal-complete` requires `GPT2_FULL_EXPECTED_VAL_LOSS` plus `GPT2_FULL_EXPECTED_HELLASWAG` so the published-metric comparison is explicit.
- [x] `scripts/run_gpt2_350M.sh`, `run_gpt2_774M.sh`, `run_gpt2_1558M.sh` — ported with ZeRO-1; larger-model scripts pass `-ge 1` to opt into the GPT-2 MLP bias+GELU epilogue path once H100 validation clears it. They pass `-x "$MAX_STEPS"` and derive `DONE_*` from that value so bounded validation runs cannot drift from their completion guards.
- [x] `scripts/pyrun_gpt2_124M.sh` — ported (PyTorch reference run). Added upstream `train_gpt2.py` reference helper; `MAX_STEPS` feeds `--num_iterations` for bounded reference runs.

## M5 — GPT-3 variants + multi-node + ZeRO 2/3 (🟡 partial)

- [x] Descriptor parser handles `gpt3:c384` … `gpt3:c12288`, including the canonical `gpt3:c5120` 13B shape. `train_gpt2cu -x 0` now has a host-only metadata dry-run path that validates GPT-2/GPT-3 descriptor parsing, checkpoint headers, parameter payload sizes, and per-tensor ZeRO-1/2 shardability before CUDA/NCCL init. The `gpt-dry` harness asserts descriptor source, channel count, and ZeRO-2 layout output for every built-in GPT-3 descriptor. **No new training C++ paths** — GPT-3 differences are flag-only.
- [x] `scripts/run_gpt3_125M.sh` — `-t 2048`, `-q 0.1`, `-sl 7.0 -sg 7.0`, `-ge 1`, with `MAX_STEPS` routed through `-x` and the matching `DONE_*` guard.
- [ ] ZeRO-2 and ZeRO-3 paths in `zero.cuh` implemented and exercised end-to-end. `-z 2` is compile-wired through the sharded optimizer/reduce-scatter path and host-only GPT/Llama dry-runs validate 8-process ZeRO-2 tensor layout plus runtime shard-count setup. `-x 0 -z 3` GPT/Llama dry-runs now validate the ZeRO-3 parameter-shard layout and local shard count. Runtime `-z 3` is compile-wired for GPT and Llama with an authoritative BF16 parameter-shard buffer, AdamW updates on the owned shard, and a compute-stream-ordered all-gather back into the full parameter layout used by the existing forward/backward kernels. Scalar NCCL all-reduces use element count `1` instead of `sizeof(float)`, shard all-gathers wait on AdamW updates from the compute stream before reading updated parameter shards, trainer update paths synchronize after the parameter all-gather before the next full-layout read, and `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh source-guards` checks the regression-prone contracts plus standalone local-shard offset coverage for GPT-2, all built-in GPT-3 descriptors, and Llama-3 1B/8B at 1/2/4/8/16 processes. H100/NCCL end-to-end validation is still pending, and the current backward/compute kernels still allocate full gradient and full compute parameter buffers.
- [x] `scripts/multi_node/run_gpt2_124M_mpi.sh` — MPI init.
- [x] `scripts/multi_node/run_gpt2_124M_fs.sbatch` — Slurm + filesystem rendezvous.
- [x] `scripts/multi_node/run_gpt2_124M_tcp.sbatch` — Slurm + TCP rendezvous.
- [x] H100-pod NCCL env vars documented in each script (`NCCL_NVLS_ENABLE=1`, `NCCL_IB_HCA=mlx5`, `NCCL_NET_GDR_LEVEL=2`, `NCCL_IB_DISABLE=0`). See [`docs/multi-gpu.md`](docs/multi-gpu.md).
- [ ] 2-node sanity run: same loss curve as single-node within the first 100 steps. `dev/compare_training_logs.py` and `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh gpt2-two-node` can launch a bounded filesystem Slurm run with `MAX_STEPS=100`, then require paired rank-0 `main.log` train-loss steps and explicit tolerance via `GPT2_TWO_NODE_REL_TOL`; the real two-node H100/NCCL evidence is still pending.

## M6 — Llama-3 1B single-node (🟡 partial)

This is the highest-risk milestone — the GQA + RoPE-fused attention kernel has no full TK reference.

- [x] `llmc/rmsnorm.cuh` + `llmc/tk/rmsnorm_tk.cuh` — RMSNorm forward + hand-written backward + fused-residual variant. Mirrors `layernorm_tk` minus mean subtraction and bias. Forward and fused-residual forward dispatch through TK for widths `{768, 1024, 1280, 1600, 2048, 4096}`; backward is a plain CUDA correctness baseline. `dev/cuda/test_rmsnorm.cu` now checks forward, fused-residual forward, `rstd`, `dinp`, and `dweight` against an independent CPU reference. Compile-checked with `make test_rmsnorm NO_MULTI_GPU=1 NO_USE_MPI=1 FORCE_NVCC_O=0`.
- [x] `llmc/rope.cuh` + `llmc/tk/rope_tk.cuh` — wrap `ThunderKittens/kernels/rotary/rotary.cu`. Backward is the inverse rotation (`sin -> -sin`). `dev/cuda/test_rope.cu` now checks HS=64 and HS=128 forward/backward against an independent CPU reference. Compile-checked with `make test_rope NO_MULTI_GPU=1 NO_USE_MPI=1 FORCE_NVCC_O=0`.
- [x] `llmc/swiglu.cuh` — plain CUDA forward/backward for `out = silu(gate) * up`. `dev/cuda/test_swiglu.cu` checks forward, `dgate`, and `dup` against an independent CPU reference. Compile-checked with `make test_swiglu NO_MULTI_GPU=1 NO_USE_MPI=1 FORCE_NVCC_O=0`.
- [ ] `llmc/attention_gqa.cuh` + `llmc/tk/attention_gqa_h100.cuh` — **new TK kernel**. TK causal forward and supported-shape TK backward paths have landed for BF16 H100 shapes, mapping query heads to shared KV heads with `n_rep = n_q / n_kv` and accumulating `dK`/`dV` into KV-head buffers. For shapes where both TK forward and TK backward are available, `attention_gqa.cuh` keeps saved Q/K unrotated and `attention_gqa_h100.cuh` applies RoPE to Q/K after the TK tile loads before WGMMA; the wrapper then inverse-rotates `dQ`/`dK` during packed-gradient unpermute before writing `dinp`. Unsupported/fallback shapes still use fused Q/K materialization and packed-gradient unpermute. `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh gqa-runtime` is the dedicated H100 gate for the CPU reference plus CUDA/TK smoke comparison. Runtime numerical validation on H100 remains pending.
- [x] `dev/cuda/test_attention_gqa.cu` — GQA + RoPE forward/backward smoke harness with an independent CPU reference for packed Llama Q/K/V, RoPE, causal GQA softmax, and packed gradients. It now covers and prints explicit success markers for a `T=128` TK-forward/fallback-backward case plus a `T=256` supported-shape TK-backward case. Compile-checked with `make test_attention_gqa NO_MULTI_GPU=1 NO_USE_MPI=1 FORCE_NVCC_O=0`; runtime is blocked locally by the CUDA driver/runtime mismatch.
- [x] `dev/validate_attention_gqa_reference.py` — CPU-only PyTorch reference check for the same `B=1 T=128` and `B=1 T=256` GQA/RoPE smoke shapes. It compares materialized-RoPE attention with repeated KV heads against a grouped/tile-load-style formulation, including backward gradients into packed Q/K/V, without initializing CUDA.
- [x] `train_llama3.cu` — sibling to `train_gpt2.cu`. Compile-ready trainer loop has landed: `LlamaConfig`, parameter layout, descriptor parser for `llama3:1B`, `llama3:8B`, `llama3.1:8B`, header magic `20240803` v5 parsing, host-side checkpoint payload-size validation, random/checkpoint initialization, Llama forward/backward/update, val/HellaSwag eval wiring, rank-0 `main.log` metric logging, AdamW, grad norm, ZeRO-0/1/2 reduction hooks, rank-0 model checkpoint files, per-rank optimizer/dataloader state files, and `-y 1` resume from the latest completed checkpoint. Host-only dry-runs use `set_zero_configs` and report the validated local ZeRO shard parameter count. Runtime validation is still blocked on H100 access and a compatible CUDA driver/runtime.
- [x] `train_llama3.py` — Python helper. Port `from_pretrained_llama3_hf` and `write_model` from `llm.c/train_llama3.py:850-920`. `dev/validate_llama3_converter.py` validates the tiny `write_model` header, BF16 payload order, and host-only C++ dry-parse path without gated HF weights.
- [x] `dev/download_llama3.py` — produces `llama3.1_8B_bf16.bin` from HF `meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B`. Requires `huggingface-cli login`; runtime conversion still needs HF access and GPU memory. Post-write validation now checks the llm.kittens header, expected payload size, hidden-dim metadata, and optionally `train_llama3cu -e CHECKPOINT -x 0`; `--cpp-zero-stage` and `--cpp-processes` route that C++ dry-run through host-only ZeRO layouts. `--write-synthetic-checkpoint` creates a tiny deterministic BF16 Llama checkpoint for repeatable parser and ZeRO layout smoke tests.
- [x] `scripts/run_llama3_1B.sh` — B=32, T=2048, LR=3e-4, warmup=2000, cosine→0.1, defaulting to Llama-tokenized FineWeb-edu 100B paths. It passes `-x "$MAX_STEPS"` and derives `DONE_*` from that value; runtime awaits H100 validation and TK GQA numerical validation.
- [x] Llama-3 dataset prep: every script in `dev/data/` supports `--model_desc llama-3`, including `hellaswag.py`. GPT-2 training shards remain uint16 magic `20240520` v1; Llama-3 training shards use uint32 magic `20240801` v7; GPT-2 eval remains magic `20240522` v1; Llama-3 eval writes `hellaswag_val_llama3.bin` with uint32 tokens and magic `20240802` v7. `dataloader.h` dispatches training and eval files by header magic. Syntax, writer, host-side artifact metadata validation, and host-side C++ loader smoke tests are wired.
- [ ] Forward+backward stable on FineWeb-edu, GQA numerics match PyTorch/reference checks on the B=1 T=128 and T=256 smoke shapes. The CPU-only PyTorch reference equivalence now passes for both shapes; `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh gqa-runtime` is wired for the H100 TK comparison, and `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh llama1b-stability` is wired for the bounded 1000-step FineWeb-edu stability run plus `main.log` validation for final train/val/eval evidence and train-loss decrease. Runtime execution is still pending.

## M7 — Llama-3 8B multi-node (🟡 partial)

8B does not fit on 8×H100-80GB without ZeRO-2 (~96 GB optimizer + params + grads before activations).

- [x] `scripts/multi_node/run_llama3_8B_fs.sbatch` — 2-node Slurm + filesystem rendezvous target for ZeRO-2. Syntax-checked; runtime awaits ZeRO-2 H100/NCCL validation, HF weights, TK GQA numerical validation, and a real two-node H100 allocation.
- [ ] HF checkpoint converter validated end-to-end (8B weights load correctly). Local validation hooks exist (`dev/validate_llama3_converter.py --cpp-validate`, `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh llama-converter-smoke`, `dev/download_llama3.py --validate-only ... --cpp-validate`, `dev/download_llama3.py --write-synthetic-checkpoint ... --cpp-validate`, `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh llama-checkpoint-smoke`, `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh llama8b-convert`, plus `LLAMA_DRY_CHECKPOINT=... scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh llama-dry`), but the real gated HF 8B conversion/load has not been run here.

## M8 — Polish (🟡 partial)

- [x] [`doc/`](doc/) (in-tree tutorial pages, mirroring llm.c's layernorm tutorial) + porting notes — `doc/` is the literate "how each kernel was ported" archive; `docs/` (this rewrite) is the operational documentation.
- [x] `profile_gpt2.cu` — port from `llm.c/profile_gpt2.cu`. Compile-checked with `make profile_gpt2cu NO_MULTI_GPU=1 NO_USE_MPI=1 FORCE_NVCC_O=0`. It accepts `--gelu-fusion 0|1` so Nsight Compute can profile both the default explicit-GELU path and the opt-in TK bias+GELU epilogue.
- [ ] `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh profile` on H100. It runs Nsight Compute through `profile_gpt2cu.py` for `PROFILE_GELU_FUSIONS="${PROFILE_GELU_FUSIONS:-0 1}"` and fails if averaged MMA tensor-core utilization is below 70% for the GEMM-heavy ops. `PROFILE_VALIDATE_ONLY=1 PROFILE_CSV_DIR=...` validates existing raw `profile_ge*.csv` exports without local `ncu`; `PROFILE_VALIDATE_ONLY=1 PROFILE_REPORT_DIR=...` validates `profile_ge*.ncu-rep` reports through the same parser/gate when local `ncu` is available for CSV export. Standalone `profile` can narrow `PROFILE_GELU_FUSIONS` for debugging, but `goal-complete` forces/requires both `profile_ge0` and `profile_ge1` evidence so the default explicit-GELU and opt-in fused epilogue paths are both covered. `dev/validate_profile_parser.py` covers the CSV parser and threshold pass/fail logic with synthetic data; the real H100 `ncu` reports are still pending.
- [ ] **Optional v1.1**: cuBLAS-style bias / GELU epilogue fusion inside the TK GEMM template. The GPT-2 MLP up-projection forward path is compile-wired behind `-ge 1`, covered by a `test_matmul` smoke case, and included in the profile matrix through `PROFILE_GELU_FUSIONS="0 1"`. `dev/validate_epilogue_source.py` keeps the TK epilogue, matmul wrapper, GPT-2 switch/fallback, profile switch, larger-model scripts, and smoke coverage aligned, but H100 numerical validation and real `ncu` profiling are still pending.
- [x] [`docs/`](docs/) tree — index, architecture, kernel-reference, build-and-run, multi-gpu, llama3, testing, precision, porting-notes, agents.
- [x] [`llms.txt`](llms.txt) and [`llms-full.txt`](llms-full.txt).
- [x] [`CHANGELOG.md`](CHANGELOG.md).
- [x] Repo-local agent skills under [`.claude/skills/`](.claude/skills/).
- [x] `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh` — executable H100 validation harness for the remaining runtime gates: preflight, compile, launch/Python syntax, source-level CUDA/NCCL/ZeRO, GQA/RoPE source-contract, BF16/H100/TK build-contract, GELU-epilogue source-contract, profile-gate source-contract, Llama conversion source-contract, rank-0 training-log evidence, launch-script, and runtime-marker contract guards, synthetic and prepared-data artifact metadata checks, host-only C++ dataloader smoke, CPU-only GQA/RoPE reference checks, dedicated GQA/RoPE CUDA runtime comparison, synthetic profile parser/threshold checks, synthetic training-log verifier checks, synthetic Llama converter writer smoke, synthetic Llama checkpoint parser smoke, synthetic GPT-2 starter-pack checks, real gated HF Llama-3.1 8B conversion/validation, CUDA runtime probe, GPT-2 starter-pack validation, kernel smoke tests, GPT-2 validation/parity, GPT/Llama dry-run/resume smoke, Llama checkpoint artifact header checks, host-only ZeRO-2/3 layout dry-run checks, standalone ZeRO shard offset validation, bounded Llama-3 1B FineWeb-edu stability, ZeRO request guard checks, ZeRO-3 runtime smoke, rank-0 training-log verification for GPT-2 smoke, Llama resume, and long runs, profiling, guarded completion, and final-artifact/log validation for full-run phases. `host-core`/`all-local` aggregates the non-CUDA-runtime host-side gates for machines without a working CUDA driver.
- [x] `dev/validate_training_log.py` — host-only `main.log` verifier for GPT-2 smoke, Llama resume, and long H100 phases. It checks final train/validation/eval steps, finite metrics, optional published/threshold values, and train-loss decrease where required after the launched job completes.
- [x] `dev/compare_training_logs.py` — host-only first-100-step loss-curve comparator for the M5 two-node sanity gate; it can require both compared curves to decrease over the selected window.
- [x] `dev/validate_log_tools.py` — synthetic host-only smoke for the training-log validator and two-node loss-curve comparator. It exercises passing logs plus failing loss-decrease, max-val-loss, expected val/eval tolerance, final-step, relative-tolerance, and non-decreasing curve cases.
- [x] `dev/validate_launch_scripts.py` — host-only launch-script guard that keeps `MAX_STEPS`, trainer step-count arguments, and `DONE_*` contracts aligned.
- [x] `dev/validate_build_contracts.py` — host-only source guard that keeps the BF16/H100/TK build contract aligned: Makefile `sm_90a`, C++20, TK include paths, `KITTENS_SM90`, `ENABLE_BF16`, no FP16/FP32/cuBLAS/cuBLASLt/cuDNN build flags, BF16 `floatX`, TK bridge static guards, 128-byte alignment, dynamic shared-memory opt-in, and the empty cuBLAS shim.
- [x] `dev/validate_epilogue_source.py` — host-only source guard that keeps the optional GPT-2 bias+GELU epilogue aligned across the TK GEMM template, `llmc/matmul.cuh`, `train_gpt2.cu` `-ge` routing/fallback, profile `--gelu-fusion`, larger-model launch scripts, and `test_matmul` smoke coverage.
- [x] `dev/validate_gqa_source.py` — host-only source guard for the custom GQA/RoPE path. It keeps tile-load RoPE routing limited to TK forward+backward shapes, checks GQA query-to-KV head mapping and shape gates, requires supported-shape TK backward to receive RoPE tables and still inverse-rotate packed `dQ`/`dK` gradients afterward, and ties the T=128 fallback-bwd/T=256 TK-bwd smoke plus CPU reference coverage to the source contract.
- [x] `dev/validate_runtime_markers.py` — host-only source guard that keeps CUDA runtime, kernel-smoke, GPT-2 validation, and GQA runtime success markers aligned with the harness assertions.
- [x] `dev/validate_training_source.py` — host-only source guard that keeps `llmc/logger.h`, both trainers, `dev/validate_training_log.py`, and the harness long-phase log-validation arguments aligned around the rank-0 `main.log` evidence format.
- [x] `dev/validate_profile_source.py` — host-only source guard that keeps `profile_gpt2cu.py`, `profile_gpt2.cu`, `dev/validate_profile_parser.py`, and the harness `profile` phase aligned around the Nsight Compute raw metrics, tensor-core utilization threshold, default/fused GELU profiling modes, validate-only CSV replay, and validate-only report mode.
- [x] `dev/validate_llama_conversion_source.py` — host-only source guard that keeps the gated Llama-3.1 8B HF alias, BF16 checkpoint header/size validation, synthetic checkpoint path, C++ dry-parse options, and `llama8b-convert` harness phase aligned.
- [x] `dev/validate_goal_harness_coverage.py` — host-only source guard that keeps compile-target coverage, unchecked runtime evidence mapping, `goal-complete` phase coverage, completion prerequisites, full-run validate-only log paths, replay negative evidence checks, and required explicit metric thresholds aligned with `goal.md`.
- [x] `dev/validate_zero_layout.py` — host-only ZeRO shard offset checker for GPT-2, every built-in GPT-3 descriptor, and Llama-3 1B/8B at 1/2/4/8/16 processes.
- [x] `dev/validate_llama_checkpoint_artifacts.py` — host-only Llama resume artifact checker. It validates `DONE_*`, model checkpoint headers, and rank state headers for expected magic/version, step, rank, and process count; `--self-test` exercises synthetic pass/fail artifact headers inside `llama-checkpoint-smoke`.

---

## Top risks (kept honest)

1. **Custom GQA + RoPE-fused attention (M6)** — no full upstream TK reference. Forward and supported-shape backward are compile-wired; TK-supported shapes now rotate Q/K in the tile-load path, while fallback shapes use fused materialization/unpermute. H100 numerical validation is still pending.
2. **GPT-2 backward runtime parity is compile-wired but unverified** — TK MHA backward and LayerNorm backward both compile, and `test_attention`/`test_layernorm` cover focused GPT kernel shapes at compile time, but MHA reduction order and LayerNorm partial-sum geometry still need real H100 parity runs.
3. **Numerical-parity tolerances** — re-derived per tensor; expect ~10× looser than llm.c on attention bwd. Document in [`docs/testing.md`](docs/testing.md).
4. **TK MHA `head_dim ∈ {64, 128}`** — adequate for GPT-2 (64) and Llama-3 (128). Any future config (HS=80, 96) needs a new TK specialization.
5. **Llama-3 8B fits poorly on 8×H100-80GB** — requires ZeRO-2 or 2-node ZeRO-1. v1's single-node Llama target is 1B; 8B is multi-node only.

## Out of scope for v1

- FP16 and FP32 activations (TK H100 GEMM/MHA are bf16-only; re-introducing them is v2 work).
- A100 / RTX 4090 / B200 backends, plus full RTX 5090 TK/model-kernel support. RTX 5090 is supported only for the generic CUDA device-test path.
- FP8 / int8 / mxfp8 (TK ships H100 kernels for these; this is the more interesting v2 story than FP16/FP32).
================================================================================
## 3. docs/architecture.md
================================================================================

# Architecture

llm.kittens is a single-binary C++/CUDA trainer. Same shape as `llm.c`, but
every tile-shaped kernel is built on ThunderKittens (TK) primitives. Element-wise
and gather/scatter kernels stay as plain CUDA — TK adds nothing on those workloads.

This page covers the **layering rule**, the file-by-file responsibility map, the
**allocator alignment** constraint, and where to put new code.

## Layering

Three layers, strictly enforced:

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A["train_gpt2.cu / train_llama3.cu<br/>(orchestration; C-style API only)"]
    B["llmc/*.cuh<br/>(C-style wrappers; thin glue + plain-CUDA verbatim ports)"]
    C["llmc/tk/*.cuh<br/>(ThunderKittens template-heavy implementations)"]
    D["ThunderKittens headers (kittens.cuh, prototype.cuh)"]
    A --> B
    B --> C
    C --> D
```

**Rule 1 — `train_*.cu` `#include`s only `llmc/*.cuh`.** Never `llmc/tk/*.cuh`
directly. The training source stays free of TK template noise.

**Rule 2 — Only files under `llmc/tk/` may `#include <kittens.cuh>` or use the
`kittens::` namespace.** This makes `llmc/tk/tk_common.cuh` the single bridge
between the C-style world and TK's template world.

**Rule 3 — `llmc/*.cuh` wrappers expose C-style function signatures.** Concrete
shape: take raw `floatX*` buffers (where `floatX = __nv_bfloat16` is locked) and
a `cudaStream_t`. No TK templates leak through this boundary.

The bridge file [`llmc/tk/tk_common.cuh`](../llmc/tk/tk_common.cuh) hard-asserts
the BF16 constraint, requires `KITTENS_SM90`, and provides:

- `llmk::bf16` aliasing `kittens::bf16` (which itself aliases `__nv_bfloat16`).
- `llmk::to_bf16(...)` reinterpret-cast helpers.
- `llmk::TK_ALIGN = 128` — the TMA-descriptor alignment constant.
- `llmk::tk_set_max_dynamic_smem(fn)` — the boilerplate `cudaFuncSetAttribute` opt-in for the full H100 dynamic shared-memory budget that TK kernels expect.

## File-by-file map

```
llm.kittens/
├── Makefile                  Build (sm_90a, c++20, BF16, TK include paths). docs/build-and-run.md.
├── README.md                 Project story.
├── goal.md                   Master TODO. Single source of truth.
├── CHANGELOG.md              Append-only history.
├── docs/                     This documentation set.
├── llms.txt                  Concise LLM index.
├── llms-full.txt             Full-tree ingestion bundle.
│
├── train_gpt2.cu             GPT-2 / GPT-3 entrypoint, compile-ready; runtime parity pending.
├── train_llama3.cu           [M6 trainer/checkpoint-resume ✓ / H100 validation pending] Llama-3 entrypoint.
├── train_gpt2.py             PyTorch reference / .bin exporter (verbatim).
├── train_llama3.py           HF→.bin converter.
├── dev/validate_gpt2_starter_pack.py Host-only GPT-2 starter-pack artifact validator.
├── dev/validate_data_artifacts.py Host-only GPT-2/Llama train/eval data artifact validator.
├── dev/validate_attention_gqa_reference.py CPU-only GQA/RoPE PyTorch reference check.
├── dev/validate_profile_parser.py Host-only profile CSV parser/threshold validator.
├── dev/validate_llama3_converter.py Host-only Llama write_model header/payload validator.
├── dev/validate_nccl_source.py Host-only source guard for brittle NCCL count, stream-order, and ZeRO runtime contracts.
├── dev/validate_build_contracts.py Host-only source guard for BF16/H100/TK build contracts.
├── dev/validate_epilogue_source.py Host-only source guard for the opt-in GPT-2 bias+GELU epilogue and profile mode.
├── dev/validate_gqa_source.py Host-only source guard for GQA/RoPE routing, gradient-unpermute, and coverage contracts.
├── dev/validate_runtime_markers.py Host-only source guard for runtime success-marker contracts.
├── dev/validate_training_source.py Host-only source guard for rank-0 training-log evidence contracts.
├── dev/validate_profile_source.py Host-only source guard for Nsight Compute profile-gate contracts.
├── dev/validate_llama_conversion_source.py Host-only source guard for Llama-3.1 8B conversion contracts.
├── dev/validate_goal_harness_coverage.py Host-only source guard for compile, goal-complete, runtime-evidence coverage, and replay negative evidence checks.
├── dev/validate_zero_layout.py Host-only ZeRO shard offset coverage.
├── dev/cuda/gpt2_validate.cu Forward-only GPT-2 loss gate, compile-ready; runtime pending.
├── test_gpt2.cu              Numerical-parity test, compile-ready; runtime parity pending.
├── profile_gpt2.cu           ncu profiling target, compile-ready; supports --gelu-fusion 0|1; runtime pending.
├── profile_gpt2cu.py         ncu post-processor, adapted to TK-only build flags; supports raw CSV input and --gelu-fusion.
├── requirements.txt          Python deps for dataset prep only.
│
├── llmc/                     Kernel + utility headers (the C-style API).
│   ├── cuda_common.h         floatX = __nv_bfloat16 (locked); compile-error on FP16/FP32.
│   ├── cuda_utils.cuh        x128, f128, stochastic_rounding (verbatim).
│   ├── cublas_common.h       Stub. cuBLAS removed in v1.
│   ├── matmul.cuh            [M2 fwd ✓ / M3 bwd ✓ / M8 epilogue compile] TK-backed GEMM wrapper + bias-grad reduce.
│   ├── attention.cuh         [M2 fwd ✓ / M3 bwd ✓] TK MHA fwd/bwd with fallback for unsupported bwd shapes.
│   ├── attention_gqa.cuh     [M6 TK fwd ✓ / supported bwd ✓] GQA + RoPE attention.
│   ├── layernorm.cuh         [M2 fwd ✓ / M3 bwd ✓] TK LayerNorm fwd + fused-residual; TK warp-sum bwd reductions.
│   ├── rmsnorm.cuh           [M6 ✓ / test_rmsnorm compile] Llama-3 RMSNorm: TK fwd + fused-residual, CUDA bwd.
│   ├── rope.cuh              [M6 ✓ / test_rope compile] Llama-3 rotary embedding wrapper.
│   ├── encoder.cuh           Token+pos embedding (verbatim plain CUDA).
│   ├── gelu.cuh              GELU (verbatim plain CUDA).
│   ├── swiglu.cuh            [M6 ✓ / test_swiglu compile] Llama-3 SwiGLU activation (plain CUDA).
│   ├── fused_classifier.cuh  Cross-entropy + softmax + dlogits (verbatim plain CUDA).
│   ├── adamw.cuh             AdamW step (verbatim plain CUDA).
│   ├── global_norm.cuh       Grad-clip norm (verbatim plain CUDA).
│   ├── zero.cuh              NCCL + ZeRO-0/1/2/3 + multi-node init; ZeRO-3 parameter shards all-gather into the full compute layout.
│   ├── dataloader.h          Distributed train/eval loaders (GPT-2/Llama header dispatch).
│   ├── tokenizer.h, sampler.h, schedulers.h, rand.h, mfu.h,
│   ├── outlier_detector.h, logger.h, utils.h    (all verbatim from llm.c)
│   └── tk/                   ThunderKittens-namespace files.
│       ├── tk_common.cuh                  Bridge: bf16 alias, TK_ALIGN, smem helper, BF16 static_assert.
│       ├── gemm_h100.cuh                  TK bf16 GEMM in header form; opt-in bias+GELU epilogue compile-wired.
│       ├── attention_h100.cuh             TK MHA fwd (`T % 192 == 0`) + bwd (`T % 256 == 0`) kernels
│       ├── attention_gqa_h100.cuh         TK GQA fwd + supported bwd [M6 partial; tile-load RoPE compile; runtime pending]
│       ├── layernorm_tk.cuh               TK LayerNorm forward + fused-residual wrappers (✓)
│       ├── rmsnorm_tk.cuh                 TK RMSNorm forward + fused-residual wrapper (✓ compile-ready)
│       └── rope_tk.cuh                    TK RoPE wrapper (✓ compile-ready)
│
├── dev/
│   ├── cuda/                 Scratch / kernel smoke tests.
│   │   ├── test_matmul.cu          GEMM forward, opt-in bias+GELU epilogue, and dWeight smoke test (✓; `make test_matmul`).
│   │   ├── test_attention.cu       GPT MHA smoke harness (✓ compile; runtime pending).
│   │   ├── test_layernorm.cu       GPT LayerNorm smoke harness (✓ compile; runtime pending).
│   │   ├── test_rope.cu            RoPE smoke harness (✓ compile; runtime pending).
│   │   ├── test_rmsnorm.cu         RMSNorm smoke harness (✓ compile; runtime pending).
│   │   ├── test_swiglu.cu          SwiGLU smoke harness (✓ compile; runtime pending).
│   │   └── test_attention_gqa.cu   GQA + RoPE smoke harness (✓ compile; runtime pending).
│   ├── data/                 Mirror of llm.c/dev/data/. Tokenizers + dataset prep.
│   ├── test_dataloader.cpp        Host-only C++ train/eval loader format smoke.
│   ├── download_starter_pack.sh   Fetches GPT-2 124M reference checkpoints.
│   ├── validate_gpt2_starter_pack.py  Checks GPT-2 tokenizer/checkpoints/debug-state without CUDA.
│   ├── validate_data_artifacts.py      Checks prepared GPT-2/Llama train/eval `.bin` files without CUDA.
│   ├── validate_attention_gqa_reference.py  Checks GQA/RoPE reference math without CUDA.
│   ├── validate_profile_parser.py      Checks profile parser thresholds without `ncu`.
│   ├── validate_llama3_converter.py    Checks Llama `write_model` header/payload order without HF weights.
│   ├── validate_nccl_source.py          Checks scalar NCCL count, all-gather stream-order, and ZeRO runtime contracts.
│   ├── validate_build_contracts.py      Checks Makefile, BF16 precision, TK bridge, and cuBLAS shim contracts.
│   ├── validate_epilogue_source.py      Checks TK/template, matmul, GPT-2 `-ge`, profile mode, launch-script, and smoke coverage for bias+GELU epilogue.
│   ├── validate_gqa_source.py           Checks GQA/RoPE tile-load routing, head mapping, TK backward gradient unpermute ordering, and smoke/reference coverage.
│   ├── validate_runtime_markers.py      Checks runtime success markers asserted by the H100 harness.
│   ├── validate_training_source.py      Checks logger/trainer/harness rank-0 training-log evidence contracts.
│   ├── validate_profile_source.py       Checks ncu metric, threshold, parser, binary, and harness profile contracts.
│   ├── validate_llama_conversion_source.py Checks HF alias, checkpoint validation, C++ dry-parse, and llama8b-convert contracts.
│   ├── validate_goal_harness_coverage.py Checks compile targets, goal-complete phases, fail-fast prerequisites, thresholds, and unchecked runtime evidence mapping.
│   ├── validate_zero_layout.py          Checks host-only ZeRO local shard offset coverage.
│   └── download_llama3.py         Converts gated HF Llama-3 weights to `.bin` and validates checkpoint headers/sizes.
│
├── scripts/                  H100×8 single-node training scripts.
│   ├── run_gpt2_124M.sh      [ported M4; runtime pending]
│   ├── run_gpt2_350M.sh      [ported M4; runtime pending]
│   ├── run_gpt2_774M.sh      [ported M4; runtime pending]
│   ├── run_gpt2_1558M.sh     [ported M4; runtime pending]
│   ├── run_gpt3_125M.sh      [ported M5; runtime pending]
│   ├── pyrun_gpt2_124M.sh    [ported M4; PyTorch reference]
│   ├── run_llama3_1B.sh      [ported M6; H100/TK GQA numerical validation pending]
│   └── multi_node/
│       ├── run_gpt2_124M_mpi.sh        [ported M5; runtime pending]
│       ├── run_gpt2_124M_fs.sbatch     [ported M5; runtime pending]
│       ├── run_gpt2_124M_tcp.sbatch    [ported M5; runtime pending]
│       └── run_llama3_8B_fs.sbatch     [ported M7; H100/TK GQA numerical validation pending]
│
├── doc/                      Tutorial / "how this kernel was ported" notes.
└── build/                    Object-file output (created at build time).
```

## How a forward step flows (target state, M2+M3)

This is the dependency shape of the intended forward+backward pass. Boxes are
kernel calls; the ones currently in the tree are bold.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph fwd["Forward (M2 target)"]
        E[encoder_forward<br/>plain CUDA verbatim] --> LN1[layernorm_forward<br/>TK]
        LN1 --> QKV["matmul_forward<br/><b>TK GEMM ✓</b>"]
        QKV --> ATT[attention_forward<br/>TK MHA]
        ATT --> PROJ["matmul_forward<br/><b>TK GEMM ✓</b>"]
        PROJ --> LN2[layernorm_forward<br/>TK]
        LN2 --> FC1["matmul_forward<br/><b>TK GEMM ✓</b><br/>(or -ge 1 epilogue)"]
        FC1 --> GELU[gelu_forward<br/>plain CUDA verbatim<br/>(default path)]
        GELU --> FC2["matmul_forward<br/><b>TK GEMM ✓</b>"]
        FC2 -.repeat per layer.-> LN1
        FC2 --> LMHEAD["matmul_forward<br/>(small_n: V%128) <b>✓</b>"]
        LMHEAD --> LOSS[fused_classifier<br/>plain CUDA verbatim]
    end
```

The backward pass (M3) reverses the same graph: `fused_classifier` produces
`dLogits`; `matmul_backward` produces `dInp`/`dWeight`/`dBias`; `attention_backward`
backprops through TK MHA for supported shapes and uses the slow CUDA fallback
otherwise; `layernorm_backward` (hand-written on TK primitives) finishes the loop.

## Allocator alignment

llm.c's parameter-tensor allocator aligns to **16 bytes** (cuBLAS requirement).
ThunderKittens' TMA descriptors require **128-byte alignment** of the underlying
global allocation. `train_gpt2.cu` now asserts 128-byte parameter tensor
offsets; the GPT-2/GPT-3 tensor shapes are naturally aligned under BF16.

The constant is exposed as `llmk::TK_ALIGN = 128` and the rounding helper as
`llmk::tk_align(bytes)` in [`llmc/tk/tk_common.cuh`](../llmc/tk/tk_common.cuh).
Use both at every parameter allocation site.

## Shared-memory opt-in

H100 kernels need `cudaFuncSetAttribute(cudaFuncAttributeMaxDynamicSharedMemorySize, ...)`
to access the full 228 KB SMEM budget. The default is 48 KB. TK kernels assume
the opt-in has been done.

`llmk::tk_set_max_dynamic_smem(kernel_fn)` does this once-per-kernel-symbol;
[`llmc/tk/gemm_h100.cuh`](../llmc/tk/gemm_h100.cuh) demonstrates the pattern with
a `static bool smem_attr_set = false;` first-call latch (see `launch<>` near the
bottom of the file).

## Where to put new code

| Adding… | Goes in | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| A new TK-backed kernel | New header in `llmc/tk/` and a thin C-style wrapper in `llmc/` | Wrapper exposes `floatX*` and `cudaStream_t`; `tk/` file may use templates and `kittens::`. |
| A plain-CUDA verbatim port from llm.c | `llmc/<name>.cuh` | Keep the comment that names the source line in llm.c. Don't simplify. |
| A test for a kernel | `dev/cuda/test_<name>.cu` | Follow the pattern in [`dev/cuda/test_matmul.cu`](../dev/cuda/test_matmul.cu): naive reference kernel + tolerance comparison. Wire the target into the Makefile. |
| Training entrypoint glue | `train_*.cu` (root) | Don't `#include` `llmc/tk/*` directly. |
| Multi-node init / NCCL | Don't add new rendezvous paths — `llmc/zero.cuh` already covers MPI / TCP / FS. ZeRO-2 is compile-wired through the sharded optimizer/reduce-scatter path; ZeRO-3 now owns a local parameter shard and all-gathers into the current full compute layout, while full compute/gradient buffers remain allocated by the kernels. |
| Dataset prep | `dev/data/*.py` | Mirror llm.c's per-script structure; honor `--model_desc gpt-2|llama-3`. |
| Training script | `scripts/run_*.sh` or `scripts/multi_node/*.{sh,sbatch}` | Mirror the corresponding llm.c script. |

## Why this layering

The training source must stay grokable. llm.c is famous because its
1900-line `train_gpt2.cu` reads top-to-bottom. TK is template-heavy; if its
templates leaked into `train_gpt2.cu`, that property would die. So we draw a
line: TK lives behind a C-style wall.
================================================================================
## 4. docs/build-and-run.md
================================================================================

# Build and run

Today the project builds **`make all`** end-to-end for the compile-ready GPT-2
path: `test_matmul`, `train_gpt2cu`, and `test_gpt2cu`. `make train_llama3cu`
also compiles the Llama trainer loop with checkpoint/resume, TK GQA forward,
and supported-shape backward where available, with CUDA fallback elsewhere.
TK-supported GQA shapes rotate Q/K inside the tile-load path, while fallback
shapes use fused materialization/unpermute. Runtime H100 parity is still pending — see
[`../goal.md`](../goal.md).

## Toolchain

| Requirement | Why |
|---|---|
| CUDA Toolkit ≥ 12.4 | nvcc with `-std=c++20` and `sm_90a`; RTX 5090 device probes use `sm_120a` |
| GCC ≥ 11 (or Clang ≥ 14) | host compiler for c++20 |
| ThunderKittens checkout | header-only; expected at `../ThunderKittens` by default |
| H100 (sm_90a) | primary runtime target for goal evidence |
| RTX 5090 (sm_120) | generic device-test target only: CUDA runtime probe plus plain CUDA SwiGLU smoke |
| NCCL ≥ 2.x + libnccl-dev | optional, multi-GPU |
| OpenMPI | optional, multi-node MPI init |
| Python ≥ 3.10 | only for `dev/data/*.py` preprocessing scripts |

`requirements.txt`: `tqdm`, `numpy<2`, `torch`, `tiktoken`, `transformers`, `datasets`, `requests`.

## TK_ROOT

The Makefile resolves the ThunderKittens checkout via `TK_ROOT`:

```bash
make                                  # default: TK_ROOT=$(abspath ../ThunderKittens)
make TK_ROOT=/path/to/ThunderKittens
```

If `$(TK_ROOT)/include/kittens.cuh` is missing the Makefile prints a warning
and the compile will fail at the first `#include <kittens.cuh>`.

## Targets

```bash
make                  # default = test_matmul + train_gpt2cu + test_gpt2cu
make test_matmul      # GEMM forward, opt-in bias+GELU epilogue, and dWeight smoke tests
make test_attention   # GPT MHA fwd/bwd smoke harness against CPU reference
make test_layernorm   # GPT LayerNorm fwd/fused/bwd smoke harness against CPU reference
make test_rope        # M6 RoPE fwd/bwd smoke harness against CPU reference
make test_rmsnorm     # M6 RMSNorm fwd/fused/bwd smoke harness against CPU reference
make test_swiglu      # M6 SwiGLU fwd/bwd smoke harness against CPU reference
make test_attention_gqa # M6 GQA + RoPE smoke harness against CPU reference
make train_gpt2cu     # GPT-2/GPT-3 trainer compile path
make gpt2_validate    # M2 forward-only GPT-2 loss gate against debug_state
make test_gpt2cu      # GPT-2 numerical parity test compile path
make train_llama3cu   # M6 Llama trainer/checkpoint-resume; TK GQA fwd/supported bwd path

# Separate optional target:
make profile_gpt2cu   # M8 profiling binary compile path

make clean            # removes binaries and build/*.o
```

The default invocation prints the build configuration banner before compiling:

```
---------------------------------------------
llm.kittens build configuration
---------------------------------------------
TK_ROOT          : /…/ThunderKittens
NVCC arch        : sm_90a
Precision        : BF16 (locked)
---------------------------------------------
✓ OpenMP found
✓ NCCL found, multi-GPU enabled
✓ MPI enabled
---------------------------------------------
```

## Build flags

The Makefile picks these defaults; override on the command line if needed.

| Flag | Default | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `TK_ROOT=` | `$(abspath ../ThunderKittens)` | ThunderKittens checkout |
| `FORCE_NVCC_O=` | `3` | nvcc optimisation level |
| `DEVICE_ARCH=SM90` | `SM90` | CUDA/TK architecture; `SM120` is available for generic RTX 5090 device probes |
| `NO_OMP=1` | unset | Disable OpenMP host parallelism |
| `NO_MULTI_GPU=1` | unset | Disable NCCL even if installed |
| `NCCL_DIR=` | unset | Enable NCCL from a custom prefix containing `include/nccl.h` and `lib*/libnccl.so` |
| `NCCL_INCLUDE_PATH=` | derived from `NCCL_DIR` when set | Override NCCL headers when using a custom install |
| `NCCL_LIB_PATH=` | derived from `NCCL_DIR` when set | Override NCCL library path when using a custom install |
| `NO_USE_MPI=1` | unset | Disable OpenMPI even if installed |
| `OPENMPI_DIR=` | `/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/openmpi` | OpenMPI prefix |

The Makefile hard-codes:

- `--use_fast_math -std=c++20`
- `--expt-extended-lambda --expt-relaxed-constexpr`
- default `DEVICE_ARCH=SM90`: `-DKITTENS_SM90 -gencode arch=compute_90a,code=sm_90a`
- opt-in `DEVICE_ARCH=SM120`: `-DKITTENS_SM120 -gencode arch=compute_120a,code=sm_120a`
- `-DENABLE_BF16`
- `-I$(TK_ROOT)/include -I$(TK_ROOT)/prototype`

`PRECISION=FP16` and `PRECISION=FP32` are not supported in v1. The compile
errors out fast — see [precision.md](precision.md).

## GPU sniff

When `nvidia-smi` is available, the Makefile prints a warning if the lowest-CC
GPU is not Hopper (sm_90):

```
⚠ Detected GPU compute capability 86; llm.kittens v1 targets sm_90a (H100).
  Build will still proceed.
```

Build still proceeds — useful when cross-compiling on a non-Hopper machine for
a Hopper deployment. Set `CI=true` to suppress the sniff.

This build-time warning is intentionally weaker than the goal harness. The
target-host `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh preflight` and `cuda-runtime` probes
target H100 / sm90-class GPUs by default. For RTX 5090 device-only checks, run
`scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh rtx5090-device`; that phase forces
`DEVICE_TEST_TARGET=rtx5090`, `DEVICE_ARCH=SM120`, and skips NCCL/MPI by
default.

## Smoke test

```bash
make test_matmul
./test_matmul

make test_attention
./test_attention

make test_layernorm
./test_layernorm
```

Expected output on an H100:

```
Device: NVIDIA H100 80GB HBM3 (sm_90)

──── 1024^3 square (default)  M=1024 N=1024 K=1024 ────
  max abs diff = 0.???  (tolerance 0.50)  PASS

──── GPT-2 124M MLP up (default)  M=4096 N=3072 K=768 ────
  max abs diff = 0.???  (tolerance 0.50)  PASS

──── GPT-2 124M LM head (small_n fallback)  M=4096 N=50304 K=768 ────
  max abs diff = 0.???  (tolerance 0.50)  PASS

──── forward bias+GELU epilogue (default)  M=1024 N=4096 K=1024 ────
  pre-GELU max abs diff = 0.????  GELU max abs diff = 0.????  (tolerance 0.50)  PASS

... dWeight overwrite and accumulated cases ...

──── 6/6 passed ────
```

Run on a non-H100 GPU prints a warning and continues (TK kernels will fail at
runtime — the test is only meaningful on an H100). On non-Hopper hardware,
treat a clean compile as the only useful signal; the goal harness preflight
still requires an H100/sm90-class target for runtime evidence.

`test_attention` checks GPT-style packed Q/K/V attention against an independent
CPU reference. It covers direct TK forward with fallback backward (`T=192`) and
padded TK forward with supported-shape TK backward (`T=256`).

`test_layernorm` checks GPT LayerNorm forward, fused residual+LayerNorm forward,
saved `mean`/`rstd`, and backward accumulation into `dinp`, `dweight`, and
`dbias` against independent CPU references.

## Quickstart (compile-ready, runtime parity pending)

The intended end-to-end command for the smallest training path is unchanged
from `llm.c`:

```bash
# 1. Reference checkpoints + tokenizer for GPT-2 124M (4 files, ~600 MB).
./dev/download_starter_pack.sh
python dev/validate_gpt2_starter_pack.py

# 2. Tokenize tiny-shakespeare for a smoke run.
python dev/data/tinyshakespeare.py
python dev/validate_data_artifacts.py

# 3. Build.
make train_gpt2cu

# 4. Train 100 steps from the bf16 124M checkpoint.
./train_gpt2cu \
    -i dev/data/tinyshakespeare/tiny_shakespeare_train.bin \
    -j dev/data/tinyshakespeare/tiny_shakespeare_val.bin \
    -e gpt2_124M_bf16.bin \
    -b 4 -t 1024 -x 100 -v 20 -s 0
```

This compiles today, but a real run still needs H100 access, the downloaded
starter pack, and the remaining parity work tracked in [`../goal.md`](../goal.md).
Add `-ge 1` to opt GPT-2's MLP up-projection into the compile-wired TK
bias+GELU epilogue after validating numerics on your H100 host.
When `-o OUTPUT_DIR` is set, rank 0 writes `OUTPUT_DIR/main.log` with
validation loss (`tel`), eval accuracy (`eval`), and train loss/LR/grad-norm
(`trl`, `lr`, `norm`) entries. The long H100 harness phases use
`dev/validate_training_log.py` to turn that file into pass/fail evidence.

## Single-node and multi-node scripts (M4–M7)

The GPT-2/GPT-3 scripts under `scripts/` now mirror their `llm.c/scripts/`
counterparts, with repo-local paths and inline H100 NCCL defaults. The Llama-3
single-node and 8B filesystem-rendezvous scripts are also present. All launch
scripts syntax-check; distributed/runtime parity is still gated on H100/NCCL
runs and TK GQA numerical validation.
The full status table is in [multi-gpu.md](multi-gpu.md#per-script-status).

## H100 goal validation

On the target H100 machine, run the bounded validation harness before checking
off any remaining runtime gate in [`../goal.md`](../goal.md):

```bash
scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh
```

The default `goal-core` phases check H100/CUDA/NCCL/MPI prerequisites, build
the compile targets, syntax-check launch/data shell scripts and Python
data/converter/profile helpers, run source-level CUDA/NCCL/ZeRO,
GQA/RoPE, BF16/H100/TK build, GELU-epilogue source, profile-gate source, Llama conversion source, rank-0 training-log evidence, launch-script, and runtime-marker contract guards, run
a tiny CUDA runtime/device-allocation probe, validate any prepared GPT-2/Llama training and HellaSwag `.bin`
artifacts found in the known data directories, run the host-only C++
DataLoader/EvalLoader smoke, run the CPU-only GQA/RoPE reference check, validate
the profile CSV parser/threshold path with synthetic data, validate the Llama
converter writer header/payload order with a tiny model, verify the GPT-2
starter-pack files, checkpoint metadata, tokenizer, and debug-state payload,
run kernel smoke tests, run GPT-2 forward/parity binaries, run GPT-2/GPT-3 and
Llama descriptor dry-runs with host-only 8-process ZeRO layout validation, write
and validate a tiny synthetic Llama checkpoint through the host-only parser,
validate host-only ZeRO-3 layouts, and check ZeRO request diagnostics.
Set `GPT_DRY_CHECKPOINT=/path/to/model.bin` when running `gpt-dry` to include
GPT checkpoint header and payload-size validation without CUDA initialization.
Set `LLAMA_DRY_CHECKPOINT=/path/to/llama.bin` when running `llama-dry` to run
the same host-only C++ checkpoint parser and payload-size validation for
converted Llama weights.
Use `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh llama-checkpoint-smoke` for a repeatable
local checkpoint parser smoke that does not need gated HF weights.
Use `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh llama-converter-smoke` to validate
`train_llama3.py::write_model` itself without gated HF weights.
Use `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh llama8b-convert` for the real gated HF
Llama-3.1 8B conversion/load gate; it validates an existing checkpoint or
converts `${LLAMA8B_MODEL:-llama3.1:8B}` and dry-parses it through the C++
ZeRO layout.
Use `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh source-guards` to check brittle source-level
NCCL contracts such as scalar all-reduce element counts, plus the ZeRO-3
parameter-shard runtime contract, launch-script step contracts, runtime success-marker
contracts, GQA/RoPE source-routing invariants, BF16/H100/TK build-contract
invariants, GELU-epilogue source contracts, profile-gate source contracts, Llama conversion source contracts,
rank-0 training-log evidence contracts, compile-target coverage, and
`goal-complete` coverage before running a distributed job.
Use `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh gqa-runtime` for the dedicated H100 GQA/RoPE
CUDA comparison against the CPU reference smoke shapes.
Set `NCCL_DIR`, `NCCL_INCLUDE_PATH`, or `NCCL_LIB_PATH` before running the
harness when the target cluster provides NCCL through a module or custom
